game_info.title: v1
game_info.concept: **Leo's Fortune:** Players control Leo, a sentient furball, navigating 24 hand-crafted platforming levels by using a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen for horizontal movement and swiping up on the right side to inflate, allowing Leo to float or roll faster, and swiping down to deflate for precise landings. The core gameplay involves solving physics-based puzzles by manipulating environmental objects, activating switches, and timing movements to avoid various traps and hazards. The objective in each level is to collect all scattered gold coins, which act as breadcrumbs left by a thief, to progress to the next stage. Upon completing all 24 levels, a Hard-core Mode is unlocked, challenging players to finish the entire game without dying, with Game Center leaderboards tracking fastest completion times for each level.
game_info.description: Take control of Leo, a charming and fuzzy sentient ball, in this physics-based platformer. Roll across treacherous terrain, inflate to float over dangerous gaps, and deflate to squeeze through tight spaces or plummet quickly to the ground. Your goal is to recover your stolen gold scattered throughout the levels and reach the exit pipe safely. Watch out for deadly spikes and bottomless pits!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left: Roll Left
Arrow Right: Roll Right
Arrow Up: Jump (tap) / Inflate and Float (hold in air)
Arrow Down: Deflate / Fast Fall / Squeeze
Space: Jump
R: Restart Level
ESC: Pause / Resume
ENTER: Start Game

TEST_1.strategy_description: The bot will move right continuously. It will detect gaps or walls ahead using a lookahead check and perform a jump (Up key) to clear obstacles, aiming for the exit coordinates.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The bot should successfully navigate the terrain, collect coins, and reach the exit pipe, triggering a level completion or game win state.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify physics stability and boundary constraints.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The bot will perform random inputs (Left, Right, Jump, Deflate) rapidly to stress-test the collision system and ensure the player does not clip through geometry or get stuck.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player entity should remain within valid game bounds and not enter an invalid state (NaN position) or get stuck inside a platform.

TEST_3.test_description: Verify hazard interaction and death state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The bot will intentionally move towards the first defined hazard (spike) in the level to trigger a collision.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game state should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE upon collision with the hazard.
